records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2151 - Whoever arrives for Hajj, performs Tawaf around the House, and prays two Rak’ahs, Allah (swt) writes for them seventy thousand good deeds, erases seventy thousand sins from them, raises them by seventy thousand degrees, grants them intercession for seventy thousand needs, and records for them the emancipation of seventy thousand slaves, each valued at ten thousand dirhams.
records.arabicText: 2151 - وَ مَنْ قَدِمَ حَاجّاً فَطَافَ بِالْبَيْتِ وَ صَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ كَتَبَ اَللَّهُ لَهُ سَبْعِينَ أَلْفَ حَسَنَةٍ وَ مَحَا عَنْهُ سَبْعِينَ أَلْفَ سَيِّئَةٍ وَ رَفَعَ لَهُ سَبْعِينَ أَلْفَ دَرَجَةٍ وَ شَفَّعَهُ فِي سَبْعِينَ أَلْفَ حَاجَةٍ وَ كَتَبَ لَهُ عِتْقَ سَبْعِينَ أَلْفَ رَقَبَةٍ قِيمَةُ كُلِّ رَقَبَةٍ عَشَرَةُ آلاَفِ دِرْهَمٍ ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2309 - It has been narrated:
"Al-Husayn ibn Ali (as) was martyred when Abu Ja'far (as) was four years old."
records.arabicText: 2309 - وَ رُوِيَ : «أَنَّهُ قُتِلَ اَلْحُسَيْنُ بْنُ عَلِيِّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ وَ لِأَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَرْبَعُ سِنِينَ ».
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Al-Husayn ibn Ali (as) was martyred when Abu Ja'far (as) was four years old."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2152 - In another narration: "This reward is for the one who performs Tawaf around the House after the sun has passed its zenith, bareheaded, barefoot, taking short steps, lowering their gaze, touching the Black Stone in every circuit without causing harm to anyone, and keeping the remembrance of Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic, continuously on their tongue."
records.arabicText: 2152 - وَ فِي خَبَرٍ آخَرَ «هَذَا اَلثَّوَابُ لِمَنْ طَافَ بِالْبَيْتِ حَتَّى تَزُولَ اَلشَّمْسُ حَاسِراً عَنْ رَأْسِهِ حَافِياً يُقَارِبُ بَيْنَ خُطَاهُ وَ يَغُضُّ بَصَرَهُ وَ يَسْتَلِمُ اَلْحَجَرَ فِي كُلِّ طَوَافٍ مِنْ غَيْرِ أَنْ يُؤْذِيَ أَحَداً وَ لاَ يَقْطَعُ ذِكْرَ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ عَنْ لِسَانِهِ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2310 - It has been narrated:
"The Kaaba complained to Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, during the period between Isa (as) and Muhammad (sw), saying, 'O Lord (azj), why have my visitors decreased? Why have those who come to me diminished?'
Allah (swt), Glorified and Exalted, revealed to it: 'I will send down a new light upon a people who will long for you as livestock long for their young and who will come to you in procession as brides are escorted to their husbands.'
This refers to the Ummah of Muhammad (sw) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family)."
records.arabicText: 2310 - وَ رُوِيَ : «أَنَّ اَلْكَعْبَةَ شَكَتْ إِلَى اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فِي اَلْفَتْرَةِ بَيْنَ عِيسَى وَ مُحَمَّدٍ صَلَوَاتُ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِمَا فَقَالَتْ يَا رَبِّ مَا لِي قَلَّ زُوَّارِي مَا لِي قَلَّ عُوَّادِي فَأَوْحَى اَللَّهُ جَلَّ جَلاَلُهُ إِلَيْهَا «أَنِّي مُنْزِلٌ نُوراً جَدِيداً عَلَى قَوْمٍ يَحِنُّونَ إِلَيْكِ كَمَا تَحِنُّ اَلْأَنْعَامُ إِلَى أَوْلاَدِهَا وَ يُزَفُّونَ إِلَيْكِ كَمَا تُزَفُّ اَلنِّسْوَانُ إِلَى أَزْوَاجِهَا» يَعْنِي أُمَّةَ مُحَمَّدٍ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ ».
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"The Kaaba complained to Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, during the period between Isa (as) and Muhammad (sw), saying, 'O Lord (azj), why have my visitors decreased? Why have those who come to me diminished?'
Allah (swt), Glorified and Exalted, revealed to it: 'I will send down a new light upon a people who will long for you as livestock long for their young and who will come to you in procession as brides are escorted to their husbands.'
This refers to the Ummah of Muhammad (sw) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family)."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2153 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic, has around the Kaaba one hundred and twenty mercies: sixty for those performing Tawaf, forty for those praying, and twenty for those merely looking at it."
records.arabicText: 2153 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «إِنَّ لِلَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ حَوْلَ اَلْكَعْبَةِ عِشْرِينَ وَ مِائَةَ رَحْمَةٍ مِنْهَا سِتُّونَ لِلطَّائِفِينَ وَ أَرْبَعُونَ لِلْمُصَلِّينَ وَ عِشْرُونَ لِلنَّاظِرِينَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2153 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic, has around the Kaaba one hundred and twenty mercies: sixty for those performing Tawaf, forty for those praying, and twenty for those merely looking at it."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2311 - Hariz narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"It was found inscribed on a stone: 'I am Allah (swt), the Lord (azj) of Bakkah. I created it on the day I created the heavens and the earth, the day I created the sun and the moon. I surrounded it with seven blessed angels, a sanctuary blessed for its people with water and milk. Its sustenance comes to it from paths above and below and through the mountain passes.'"
records.arabicText: 2311 - وَ رَوَى حَرِيزٌ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «وُجِدَ فِي حَجَرٍ «أَنِّي أَنَا اَللَّهُ ذُو بَكَّةَ صَنَعْتُهَا يَوْمَ خَلَقْتُ اَلسَّمَاوَاتِ وَ اَلْأَرْضَ وَ يَوْمَ خَلَقْتُ اَلشَّمْسَ وَ اَلْقَمَرَ وَ حَفَفْتُهَا بِسَبْعَةِ أَمْلاَكٍ حَفّاً مُبَارَكٌ لِأَهْلِهَا فِي اَلْمَاءِ وَ اَللَّبَنِ يَأْتِيهَا رِزْقُهَا مِنْ سُبُلٍ مِنْ أَعْلاَهَا وَ أَسْفَلِهَا وَ اَلثَّنِيَّةِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2311 - Hariz narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It was found inscribed on a stone: 'I am Allah (swt), the Lord (azj) of Bakkah. I created it on the day I created the heavens and the earth, the day I created the sun and the moon. I surrounded it with seven blessed angels, a sanctuary blessed for its people with water and milk. Its sustenance comes to it from paths above and below and through the mountain passes.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2154 - It has been narrated: "Whoever performs Tawaf around the House is cleansed of their sins."
records.arabicText: 2154 - وَ رُوِيَ : «أَنَّ مَنْ طَافَ بِالْبَيْتِ خَرَجَ مِنْ ذُنُوبِهِ ».
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever performs Tawaf around the House is cleansed of their sins."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2312 - It has been narrated:
"It was found inscribed on another stone: 'This is the Sacred House of Allah (swt) in Mecca. Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, has guaranteed the sustenance of its people through three blessed means. It is blessed for its people in meat and water.'"
records.arabicText: 2312 - وَ رُوِيَ : «أَنَّهُ وُجِدَ فِي حَجَرٍ آخَرَ مَكْتُوبٌ هَذَا بَيْتُ اَللَّهِ اَلْحَرَامُ بِمَكَّةَ تَكَفَّلَ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ بِرِزْقِ أَهْلِهِ مِنْ ثَلاَثَةِ سُبُلٍ مُبَارَكٌ لِأَهْلِهِ فِي اَللَّحْمِ وَ اَلْمَاءِ.
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It was found inscribed on another stone: 'This is the Sacred House of Allah (swt) in Mecca. Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, has guaranteed the sustenance of its people through three blessed means. It is blessed for its people in meat and water.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2155 - Abu Ja’far (as) said:
"Whoever prays two Rak’ahs at the Station of Ibrahim, it is equivalent to freeing six souls."
records.arabicText: 2155 - وَ قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «مَنْ صَلَّى عِنْدَ اَلْمَقَامِ رَكْعَتَيْنِ عَدَلَتَا عِتْقَ سِتِّ نَسَمَاتٍ.
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever prays two Rak’ahs at the Station of Ibrahim, it is equivalent to freeing six souls."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2156 - A single Tawaf performed before Hajj is better than seventy Tawafs performed after Hajj.
records.arabicText: 2156 - وَ طَوَافٌ قَبْلَ اَلْحَجِّ أَفْضَلُ مِنْ سَبْعِينَ طَوَافاً بَعْدَ اَلْحَجِّ ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2313 - It has been narrated from Abu Hamzah Al-Thumali, who said:
Imam Ali ibn Al-Husayn (as) said to us: "Which is the most virtuous place?"
We replied: "Allah (swt), His Messenger, and the son of His Messenger know best."
Imam (as) said: "The most virtuous place is what lies between the Rukn (corner of the Kaaba) and the Maqam (Station of Ibrahim). If a man were to live as long as Nuh (as) did among his people—one thousand years less fifty—fasting by day and praying by night in that place, and then met Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, without our Wilayah (allegiance), it would not benefit him in any way."
records.arabicText: 2313 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ اَلثُّمَالِيِّ قَالَ قَالَ لَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ اَلْحُسَيْنِ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ : «أَيُّ اَلْبِقَاعِ أَفْضَلُ» فَقُلْنَا اَللَّهُ وَ رَسُولُهُ وَ اِبْنُ رَسُولِهِ أَعْلَمُ فَقَالَ «أَمَّا أَفْضَلُ اَلْبِقَاعِ مَا بَيْنَ اَلرُّكْنِ وَ اَلْمَقَامِ وَ لَوْ أَنَّ رَجُلاً عُمِّرَ مَا عُمِّرَ نُوحٌ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فِي قَوْمِهِ أَلْفَ سَنَةٍ إِلاَّ خَمْسِينَ عَاماً يَصُومُ اَلنَّهَارَ وَ يَقُومُ اَللَّيْلَ فِي ذَلِكَ اَلْمَكَانِ ثُمَّ لَقِيَ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ بِغَيْرِ، وَلاَيَتِنَا لَمْ يَنْفَعْهُ ذَلِكَ شَيْئاً».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2313 - It has been narrated from Abu Hamzah Al-Thumali, who said:
Imam Ali ibn Al-Husayn (as) said to us:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Which is the most virtuous place?"
We replied: "Allah (swt), His Messenger, and the son of His Messenger know best."
Imam (as) said: "The most virtuous place is what lies between the Rukn (corner of the Kaaba) and the Maqam (Station of Ibrahim). If a man were to live as long as Nuh (as) did among his people—one thousand years less fifty—fasting by day and praying by night in that place, and then met Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, without our Wilayah (allegiance), it would not benefit him in any way."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2157 - Whoever resides in Makkah for one year, performing Tawaf is better for them than prayer. Whoever resides for two years should alternate between Tawaf and prayer. Whoever resides for three years, prayer becomes better for them.
records.arabicText: 2157 - وَ مَنْ أَقَامَ بِمَكَّةَ سَنَةً فَالطَّوَافُ أَفْضَلُ لَهُ مِنَ اَلصَّلاَةِ وَ مَنْ أَقَامَ سَنَتَيْنِ خَلَطَ مِنْ ذَا وَ ذَا وَ مَنْ أَقَامَ ثَلاَثَ سِنِينَ كَانَتِ اَلصَّلاَةُ أَفْضَلَ لَهُ ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2314 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said on the day of the conquest of Mecca:
"Indeed, Allah (swt), Blessed and Exalted, sanctified Mecca 'on the day He created the heavens and the earth.' It is sacred until the Day of Resurrection. It was not made lawful for anyone before me, nor will it be made lawful for anyone after me. It was only made lawful for me for one hour of the day."
records.arabicText: 2314 - وَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ يَوْمَ فَتْحِ مَكَّةَ : «إِنَّ اَللَّهَ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى حَرَّمَ مَكَّةَ «يَوْمَ خَلَقَ اَلسَّمٰاوٰاتِ وَ اَلْأَرْضَ» فَهِيَ حَرَامٌ إِلَى أَنْ تَقُومَ اَلسَّاعَةُ لَمْ تَحِلَّ لِأَحَدٍ قَبْلِي وَ لاَ تَحِلُّ لِأَحَدٍ مِنْ بَعْدِي وَ لَمْ تَحِلَّ لِي إِلاَّ سَاعَةً مِنَ اَلنَّهَارِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2314 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said on the day of the conquest of Mecca:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Indeed, Allah (swt), Blessed and Exalted, sanctified Mecca 'on the day He created the heavens and the earth.' It is sacred until the Day of Resurrection. It was not made lawful for anyone before me, nor will it be made lawful for anyone after me. It was only made lawful for me for one hour of the day."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2315 - Kulayb Al-Asadi narrated from Abu Abdullah (as):
"The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) sought permission from Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, regarding Mecca three times over the course of time. Allah (swt) granted him permission for it during one hour of the day, after which He made it sacred as long as the heavens and the earth remain."
records.arabicText: 2315 - وَ رَوَى كُلَيْبٌ اَلْأَسَدِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «أَنَّ رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ اِسْتَأْذَنَ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فِي مَكَّةَ ثَلاَثَ مَرَّاتٍ مِنَ اَلدَّهْرِ فَأَذِنَ اَللَّهُ لَهُ فِيهَا سَاعَةً مِنَ اَلنَّهَارِ ثُمَّ جَعَلَهَا حَرَاماً مَا دَامَتِ اَلسَّمَاوَاتُ وَ اَلْأَرْضُ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2315 - Kulayb Al-Asadi narrated from Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) sought permission from Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, regarding Mecca three times over the course of time. Allah (swt) granted him permission for it during one hour of the day, after which He made it sacred as long as the heavens and the earth remain."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2158 - It has been narrated: "For those who are not residents of Makkah, performing Tawaf is better than prayer, while for the residents of Makkah, prayer is better."
records.arabicText:
2158 - وَ رُوِيَ : «أَنَّ اَلطَّوَافَ لِغَيْرِ أَهْلِ مَكَّةَ أَفْضَلُ مِنَ اَلصَّلاَةِ وَ اَلصَّلاَةُ لِأَهْلِ مَكَّةَ أَفْضَلُ ».
ومن كان مع قوم وحفظ عليهم رحلهم حتى يطوفوا أو يسعوا كان أعظمهم أجرا.

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2316 - He {as.} said:
"Indeed, Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, sanctified Mecca 'on the day He created the heavens and the earth.' Its vegetation is not to be uprooted, its trees are not to be cut, its game is not to be disturbed, and its lost property is not to be picked up except by one who announces it."
Al-Abbas ibn Abd al-Muttalib stood and said: "O Messenger of Allah (swt), except for Al-Idhkhir (a type of grass), for it is used in graves and the roofs of our homes."
The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) remained silent for a moment, and Al-Abbas regretted what he had said.
Then the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said: "Except for Al-Idhkhir."
records.arabicText: 2316 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «إِنَّ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ حَرَّمَ مَكَّةَ «يَوْمَ خَلَقَ اَلسَّمٰاوٰاتِ وَ اَلْأَرْضَ» وَ لاَ يُخْتَلَى خَلاَهَا وَ لاَ يُعْضَدُ شَجَرُهَا وَ لاَ يُنَفَّرُ صَيْدُهَا وَ لاَ يُلْتَقَطُ لُقَطَتُهَا إِلاَّ لِمُنْشِدٍ فَقَامَ إِلَيْهِ اَلْعَبَّاسُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اَلْمُطَّلِبِ فَقَالَ يَا رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ إِلاَّ اَلْإِذْخِرُ فَإِنَّهُ لِلْقَبْرِ وَ لِسُقُوفِ بُيُوتِنَا فَسَكَتَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ سَاعَةً وَ نَدِمَ اَلْعَبَّاسُ عَلَى مَا قَالَ ثُمَّ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ «إِلاَّ اَلْإِذْخِرُ» ».
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Indeed, Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, sanctified Mecca 'on the day He created the heavens and the earth.' Its vegetation is not to be uprooted, its trees are not to be cut, its game is not to be disturbed, and its lost property is not to be picked up except by one who announces it."
Al-Abbas ibn Abd al-Muttalib stood and said: "O Messenger of Allah (swt), except for Al-Idhkhir (a type of grass), for it is used in graves and the roofs of our homes."
The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) remained silent for a moment, and Al-Abbas regretted what he had said.
Then the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said: "Except for Al-Idhkhir."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2159 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said: "Fulfilling the need of a believer is better than performing Tawaf, and Tawaf, and Tawaf—up to ten times."
records.arabicText: 2159 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «قَضَاءُ حَاجَةِ اَلْمُؤْمِنِ أَفْضَلُ مِنْ طَوَافٍ وَ طَوَافٍ وَ طَوَافٍ حَتَّى عَدَّ عَشْراً ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2159 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Fulfilling the need of a believer is better than performing Tawaf, and Tawaf, and Tawaf—up to ten times."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2317 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"The foundation of the House (Kaaba) extends from the seventh layer of the lower earth to the seventh layer of the upper earth."
records.arabicText: 2317 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «أَسَاسُ اَلْبَيْتِ مِنَ اَلْأَرْضِ اَلسَّابِعَةِ اَلسُّفْلَى إِلَى اَلْأَرْضِ اَلسَّابِعَةِ اَلْعُلْيَا».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2317 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The foundation of the House (Kaaba) extends from the seventh layer of the lower earth to the seventh layer of the upper earth."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2160 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said: "The Yemeni Corner is our gate through which we enter Paradise."
records.arabicText: 2160 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : « اَلرُّكْنُ اَلْيَمَانِيُّ بَابُنَا اَلَّذِي نَدْخُلُ مِنْهُ اَلْجَنَّةَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2160 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The Yemeni Corner is our gate through which we enter Paradise."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2161 - Imam (as) said: "Within it is a gate of Paradise that has never been closed since it was opened."
records.arabicText: 2161 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «فِيهِ بَابٌ مِنْ أَبْوَابِ اَلْجَنَّةِ لَمْ يُغْلَقْ مُنْذُ فُتِحَ ».
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Within it is a gate of Paradise that has never been closed since it was opened."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2318 - Abu Hammam Isma'il ibn Hammam narrated from Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as):
Imam (as) said to a man: "What do you understand 'Sakina' to mean?"
The people did not know what it was and replied: "May we be your ransom, what is it?"
Imam (as) said: "'Sakina' is a breeze that comes from Paradise, fragrant, and has a form like that of a human. It accompanies the Prophets (as). It is the same 'Sakina' that was sent down upon Prophet Ibrahim (as) when he was building the Kaaba. It guided him by pointing out this and that, and he built the foundation upon it."
records.arabicText: 2318 - وَ رَوَى أَبُو هَمَّامٍ إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ هَمَّامٍ عَنِ اَلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُ قَالَ لِرَجُلٍ «أَيُّ شَيْءٍ اَلسَّكِينَةُ عِنْدَكُمْ» فَلَمْ يَدْرِ اَلْقَوْمُ مَا هِيَ فَقَالُوا جَعَلَنَا اَللَّهُ فِدَاكَ مَا هِيَ قَالَ «رِيحٌ تَخْرُجُ مِنَ اَلْجَنَّةِ طَيِّبَةٌ لَهَا صُورَةٌ كَصُورَةِ اَلْإِنْسَانِ تَكُونُ مَعَ اَلْأَنْبِيَاءِ عَلَيْهِمُ اَلسَّلاَمُ وَ هِيَ اَلَّتِي أُنْزِلَتْ عَلَى إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ حِينَ بَنَى اَلْكَعْبَةَ فَأَخَذَتْ تَأْخُذُ كَذَا وَ كَذَا وَ بَنَى اَلْأَسَاسَ عَلَيْهَا».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2318 - Abu Hammam Isma'il ibn Hammam narrated from Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Imam (as) said to a man: "What do you understand 'Sakina' to mean?"
The people did not know what it was and replied: "May we be your ransom, what is it?"
Imam (as) said: "'Sakina' is a breeze that comes from Paradise, fragrant, and has a form like that of a human. It accompanies the Prophets (as). It is the same 'Sakina' that was sent down upon Prophet Ibrahim (as) when he was building the Kaaba. It guided him by pointing out this and that, and he built the foundation upon it."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2162 - "Within it is a river from Paradise into which the deeds of the servants are cast.”
records.arabicText: 2162 - وَ فِيهِ نَهَرٌ مِنَ اَلْجَنَّةِ يُلْقَى فِيهِ أَعْمَالُ اَلْعِبَادِ ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2319 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"The height of the Kaaba was nine cubits, and it had no roof. The Quraysh then roofed it, raising its height to eighteen cubits. Later, Al-Hajjaj demolished it during his conflict with Ibn Al-Zubayr and rebuilt it, raising its height to twenty-seven cubits."
records.arabicText: 2319 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «كَانَ طُولُ اَلْكَعْبَةِ تِسْعَةَ أَذْرُعٍ وَ لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهَا سَقْفٌ فَسَقَّفَهَا قُرَيْشٌ ثَمَانِيَةَ عَشَرَ ذِرَاعاً ثُمَّ كَسَرَهَا اَلْحَجَّاجُ عَلَى اِبْنِ اَلزُّبَيْرِ فَبَنَاهَا وَ جَعَلَهَا سَبْعَةً وَ عِشْرِينَ ذِرَاعاً ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2319 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The height of the Kaaba was nine cubits, and it had no roof. The Quraysh then roofed it, raising its height to eighteen cubits. Later, Al-Hajjaj demolished it during his conflict with Ibn Al-Zubayr and rebuilt it, raising its height to twenty-seven cubits."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2320 - It has been narrated from Sa'id ibn Abdullah Al-A'raj, from Abu Abdullah (as), that he said:
"In the pre-Islamic era, the Quraysh demolished the Kaaba. When they wanted to rebuild it, they were prevented from doing so by a sense of fear that filled their hearts. Then someone among them said, 'Let each of you bring the purest of his wealth, and do not bring any wealth gained from severing family ties or from unlawful means.' They did so, and the fear was lifted, allowing them to proceed with its construction.
When they reached the location of the Black Stone, they disputed among themselves as to who would place it in its position, and their disagreement nearly led to conflict. They agreed to let the first person to enter the mosque decide.
The first to enter was the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family). When he came to them, he asked for a cloth to be spread out. He placed the Black Stone in the middle of it and then instructed the tribes to hold the edges of the cloth and lift it together. Once it was raised, the Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) took the stone with his own hands and placed it in its position.
Thus, Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, honored him with this distinction."
records.arabicText: 2320 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ اَلْأَعْرَجِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : «إِنَّ قُرَيْشاً فِي اَلْجَاهِلِيَّةِ هَدَمُوا اَلْبَيْتَ فَلَمَّا أَرَادُوا بِنَاءَهُ حِيلَ بَيْنَهُ وَ بَيْنَهُمْ وَ أُلْقِيَ فِي رُوعِهِمُ اَلرُّعْبُ حَتَّى قَالَ قَائِلٌ مِنْهُمْ لِيَأْتِ كُلُّ رَجُلٍ مِنْكُمْ بِأَطْيَبِ مَالِهِ وَ لاَ تَأْتُوا بِمَالٍ اِكْتَسَبْتُمُوهُ مِنْ قَطِيعَةِ رَحِمٍ أَوْ حَرَامٍ فَفَعَلُوا فَخُلِّيَ بَيْنَهُمْ وَ بَيْنَ بُنْيَانِهِ فَبَنَوْهُ حَتَّى اِنْتَهَوْا إِلَى مَوْضِعِ اَلْحَجَرِ اَلْأَسْوَدِ فَتَشَاجَرُوا فِيهِ أَيُّهُمْ يَضَعُ اَلْحَجَرَ فِي مَوْضِعِهِ حَتَّى كَادَ أَنْ يَكُونَ بَيْنَهُمْ شَرٌّ فَحَكَّمُوا أَوَّلَ مَنْ يَدْخُلُ مِنْ بَابِ اَلْمَسْجِدِ فَدَخَلَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ فَلَمَّا أَتَاهُمْ أَمَرَ بِثَوْبٍ فَبَسَطَ ثُمَّ وَضَعَ اَلْحَجَرَ فِي وَسَطِهِ ثُمَّ أَخَذَتِ اَلْقَبَائِلُ بِجَوَانِبِ اَلثَّوْبِ فَرَفَعُوهُ ثُمَّ تَنَاوَلَهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَوَضَعَهُ فِي مَوْضِعِهِ فَخَصَّهُ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ بِهِ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2320 - It has been narrated from Sa'id ibn Abdullah Al-A'raj, from Abu Abdullah (as), that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"In the pre-Islamic era, the Quraysh demolished the Kaaba. When they wanted to rebuild it, they were prevented from doing so by a sense of fear that filled their hearts. Then someone among them said, 'Let each of you bring the purest of his wealth, and do not bring any wealth gained from severing family ties or from unlawful means.' They did so, and the fear was lifted, allowing them to proceed with its construction.
When they reached the location of the Black Stone, they disputed among themselves as to who would place it in its position, and their disagreement nearly led to conflict. They agreed to let the first person to enter the mosque decide.
The first to enter was the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family). When he came to them, he asked for a cloth to be spread out. He placed the Black Stone in the middle of it and then instructed the tribes to hold the edges of the cloth and lift it together. Once it was raised, the Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) took the stone with his own hands and placed it in its position.
Thus, Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, honored him with this distinction."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2163 - It has been narrated: "It is the right hand of Allah (swt) on His earth, with which He shakes hands with His creation."
records.arabicText: 2163 - وَ رُوِيَ : «أَنَّهُ يَمِينُ اَللَّهِ فِي أَرْضِهِ يُصَافِحُ بِهَا خَلْقَهُ ».
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It is the right hand of Allah (swt) on His earth, with which He shakes hands with His creation."
